title: Thats No Lady Thats My Cousin

jokes.text: Oh, check it out. LAPD, The Home Game.
jokes.setup: Family gathering with video camera recording
jokes.punchline: Comparing family video to police reality TV show
jokes.explanation: Sharp pop culture reference with good economy - Will's street-smart perspective on family dynamics

jokes.text: I have fallen and I can't get up.
jokes.setup: Will handling the video camera
jokes.punchline: Reference to famous Life Alert commercial while presumably on the ground
jokes.explanation: Predictable commercial reference but fits Will's character to make light of situations

jokes.text: Okay, but I'm living with the one who keeps the house.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil about to announce important family news
jokes.punchline: Will assumes divorce and pragmatically picks sides
jokes.explanation: Great character-driven humor showing Will's street-smart pragmatism and misreading of situation

jokes.text: No. We're not getting a divorce, stupid.
jokes.setup: Will's assumption about divorce
jokes.punchline: Vivian's blunt correction calling him stupid
jokes.explanation: Functional punchline that works off Will's setup, Vivian's directness fits character

jokes.text: Wait. Hold it, hold it, hold it. You and you?
jokes.setup: Announcement of pregnancy
jokes.punchline: Will's grossed-out reaction to his aunt and uncle having sex
jokes.explanation: Natural teen reaction to relatives' sexuality, good physical comedy timing

jokes.text: Yo, they ain't laughing, y'all.
jokes.setup: Family's serious reaction to pregnancy news
jokes.punchline: Will acknowledging the lack of audience laughter
jokes.explanation: Classic Fresh Prince meta moment - Will breaking fourth wall fits his character perfectly

jokes.text: Ew! And I'm not baby-sitting. I'm not changing diapers. And I'm not sharing my trust fund.
jokes.setup: Family pregnancy announcement
jokes.punchline: Hilary's selfish concerns escalating to money
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary characterization - escalating from reasonable concerns to pure selfishness about trust fund

jokes.text: Now, kids, I have great news. We're going to have a baby! Yay.
jokes.setup: Phil's second attempt at announcing pregnancy
jokes.punchline: Forced, unenthusiastic 'Yay' from the kids
jokes.explanation: Solid family sitcom moment showing forced enthusiasm after initial shock

jokes.text: Going to the electric chair?
jokes.setup: Vivian's appearance (presumably disheveled from pregnancy)
jokes.punchline: Comparing her look to someone facing execution
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag setup with dark humor twist, economical delivery

jokes.text: The catering business is so unfair. I mean, a couple of nuns get botulism and all of a sudden, I'm blacklisted.
jokes.setup: Hilary complaining about her catering business
jokes.punchline: Dismissing food poisoning nuns as minor inconvenience
jokes.explanation: Excellent Hilary moment - perfect absurdist understatement of serious food poisoning incident, highly quotable

jokes.text: Wait a minute. I have to move out? Whose fault is it that she's pregnant?
jokes.setup: Being told she needs to move out for baby's room
jokes.punchline: Blaming Vivian for being pregnant
jokes.explanation: Classic Hilary selfishness and twisted logic - making pregnancy sound like Vivian's personal failing

jokes.text: Yes, and we don't want you in any of them.
jokes.setup: Hilary pointing out they have three extra bedrooms
jokes.punchline: Phil's blunt rejection
jokes.explanation: Great deadpan delivery from Phil, economical and harsh - fits his exasperation with Hilary

jokes.text: Hilary, honey, what your father's trying to say is... you don't live here anymore, honey.
jokes.setup: Vivian appearing to soften Phil's harsh words
jokes.punchline: Saying the exact same thing more sweetly
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection - audience expects Vivian to be diplomatic but she's just as direct

jokes.text: But this is all so sudden. I mean, like, if I'd only had some kind of warning or something.
jokes.setup: Hilary acting shocked about moving out
jokes.punchline: Implying she should have been warned about the pregnancy affecting her
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Hilary's self-centeredness - expecting advance notice of family changes

jokes.text: Again?
jokes.setup: Being told to get a job
jokes.punchline: Treating employment like an unreasonable recurring burden
jokes.explanation: Perfect single-word punchline showing Hilary's privileged attitude toward work - maximum economy

jokes.text: What a week. First, those damn nuns, now this.
jokes.setup: References earlier botulism incident
jokes.punchline: Equating food poisoning nuns with having to move out
jokes.explanation: Nice callback tying her business failure to personal inconvenience - shows warped priorities

jokes.text: A word to the wise, Will: Stay in high school forever.
jokes.setup: Hilary's adult struggles
jokes.punchline: Advising Will to avoid adulthood entirely
jokes.explanation: Solid advice joke with irony - coming from someone who never really grew up

jokes.text: Bet.
jokes.setup: Hilary's advice about staying in school
jokes.punchline: Will's dry, single-word agreement
jokes.explanation: Perfect economical response - Will's street slang acknowledging the wisdom in Hilary's desperation

jokes.text: Just do what Carlton does: Give them money.
jokes.setup: Ashley worried about making friends at new school
jokes.punchline: Will's cynical advice based on Carlton's social strategy
jokes.explanation: Good character-driven humor - Will's street wisdom about Carlton's privileged approach to friendship

jokes.text: Oh, great, now I'm ugly.
jokes.setup: Vivian complimenting Ashley's personality
jokes.punchline: Ashley interpreting personality praise as looks insult
jokes.explanation: Smart teenage logic joke - Ashley's insecurity reading between lines of well-meant compliment

jokes.text: But if we run into each other at school, act like we've never met. Don't acknowledge me, don't talk to me, act like I'm invisible. If you're not sure how, just do what all the other girls do.
jokes.setup: Carlton giving Ashley instructions about school behavior
jokes.punchline: Self-aware joke about his unpopularity with girls
jokes.explanation: Excellent self-aware humor from Carlton - long setup pays off with self-deprecating punchline about rejection

jokes.text: And any day now, it's going to kick in, right?
jokes.setup: Carlton claiming he's worked hard at becoming popular
jokes.punchline: Admitting his popularity hasn't actually materialized yet
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Carlton's delusional optimism about his social status

jokes.text: Cute, rich girls that know the one thing their parents can't stand... is for them to be dating a big black dude.
jokes.setup: Will excited about cute rich girls at prep school
jokes.punchline: Cynical observation about rebellious rich girls and racial dynamics
jokes.explanation: Smart social commentary disguised as dating advice - Will's street-smart read on prep school dynamics

jokes.text: Just look for the huddle of girls with the chocolate center.
jokes.setup: Ashley asking to hang out with Will
jokes.punchline: Describing himself and his admirers as candy with chocolate center
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay combining racial identity with candy metaphor - shows Will's confidence and humor

jokes.text: It's a disgrace. It's a scandal. It's an outrage! It's the mother of my children.
jokes.setup: Phil's dramatic complaints building up
jokes.punchline: Revealing he's talking about his pregnant wife
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and misdirection - dramatic buildup leads to affectionate identification

jokes.text: Hurt me! Hurt me!
jokes.setup: Will telling Carlton to relax around girls
jokes.punchline: Carlton's inappropriate sexual outburst
jokes.explanation: Typical Carlton inappropriate sexual comment - fits character but relies on shock value

jokes.text: Hold up, baby. See... now, I noticed you noticing me. So I just want to put you on notice that I noticed you, too.
jokes.setup: Will approaching attractive girl Veronica
jokes.punchline: Overly complex wordplay pickup line with 'notice'
jokes.explanation: Classic Will pickup line with clever wordplay - shows his verbal dexterity and confidence

jokes.text: Actually, baby, you got it, but I take donations.
jokes.setup: Veronica asking if that's what passes for a compliment
jokes.punchline: Will twisting her 'got it' into sexual innuendo about donations
jokes.explanation: Decent comeback with sexual wordplay but somewhat crude - fits Will's character

jokes.text: You drug them?
jokes.setup: Will saying his success with women is 'a chemical thing'
jokes.punchline: Kenny's literal interpretation as drugging
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection joke with efficient delivery - Kenny's innocent literal interpretation

jokes.text: Will, the judges like her. They really, really like her.
jokes.setup: Carlton watching attractive girls with Will
jokes.punchline: Olympic judging reference about girl's attractiveness
jokes.explanation: Standard pop culture reference joke - Olympics judging applied to girl-watching

jokes.text: I can't believe how hard it is to find a beachfront place... with a pool and tennis court for $500 a month. I mean, what's happening to this country? It's enough to make me want to vote.
jokes.setup: Hilary apartment hunting on tiny budget
jokes.punchline: Unrealistic expectations and treating voting as extreme measure
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary moment - completely unrealistic expectations and political apathy as punchline

jokes.text: You're not going to be happy until I'm living in a trailer, eating Spam on Ritz. I mean, that's where this is going, isn't it?
jokes.setup: Suggestion to lower apartment standards
jokes.punchline: Hilary's vision of rock-bottom living
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Hilary's privileged perspective - Spam on Ritz as poverty food

jokes.text: Run with that.
jokes.setup: Hilary thinking they want to get rid of her
jokes.punchline: Phil encouraging her correct suspicion
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery - Phil not even pretending to deny wanting her gone

jokes.text: And that was just from 10 minutes of hiding under the bleachers.
jokes.setup: Carlton talking about learning his assumptions about women were wrong
jokes.punchline: Revealing he was literally spying on girls
jokes.explanation: Classic Carlton inappropriate behavior revelation - good setup/punchline structure

jokes.text: Boom Boom LaSalle. I wonder why they called her that?
jokes.setup: Phil mentioning stripper named Boom Boom LaSalle
jokes.punchline: Will's mock-innocent question about the obvious name
jokes.explanation: Standard sexual innuendo joke - Will playing innocent about obvious stripper name

jokes.text: You the man.
jokes.setup: Phil's story about stripper encounter
jokes.punchline: Will's enthusiastic approval of Uncle Phil's story
jokes.explanation: Simple reaction beat - Will's street slang approval of Phil's college story

jokes.text: finished his Yoo-hoo and went right home.
jokes.setup: Phil's seemingly salacious stripper story
jokes.punchline: Innocent ending with chocolate drink and going home
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection - builds up sexy story then deflates with innocent childhood behavior

jokes.text: Wait a minute, Philip. Go back to where you finished your Yoo-hoo.
jokes.setup: Phil's innocent story ending
jokes.punchline: Vivian wanting more details about the 'innocent' part
jokes.explanation: Good callback playing on the innocent/sexual double meaning of Phil's story

jokes.text: Uncle Phil, you'll be happy to know that... none of the boys tried nothing with me, neither.
jokes.setup: Phil asking Ashley about boys trying anything
jokes.punchline: Will's double negative implying boys DID try something
jokes.explanation: Clever grammar joke - Will's street dialect double negative accidentally admits to romantic attention

jokes.text: There you go noticing me again.
jokes.setup: Will spotting Veronica again
jokes.punchline: Callback to his earlier 'noticing' pickup line
jokes.explanation: Good callback to establish running gag - Will persistent with his wordplay approach

jokes.text: She senses that big, wet, nasty blob of mayonnaise on your cheek... and she can't eat till you wipe it off.
jokes.setup: Will claiming girl wants him due to raw animal power
jokes.punchline: Carlton's gross visual explanation for her stare
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of Will's ego with gross visual detail - Carlton's practical observation

jokes.text: It's kind of hard to impress the honeys... when your baby sister's blowing bubbles in your milk.
jokes.setup: Carlton not wanting Ashley to sit with him
jokes.punchline: Specific embarrassing behavior that ruins his image
jokes.explanation: Good specific visual that explains Carlton's embarrassment - childish behavior ruining cool image

jokes.text: She's cool! She's hot! She's... your baby sister, man!
jokes.setup: Carlton getting excited about attractive girl
jokes.punchline: Will revealing it's transformed Ashley
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and revelation structure - Carlton's enthusiasm crashed by family reality

jokes.text: I'm ordering you to look unattractive.
jokes.setup: Carlton is upset that Ashley is getting male attention
jokes.punchline: His impossible demand that she become ugly on command
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist command that shows Carlton's controlling nature and impossible expectations

jokes.text: Ashley, who did you have for breakfast, some hormones or something?
jokes.setup: Ashley's sudden transformation and mature behavior
jokes.punchline: Will's crude observation about puberty
jokes.explanation: Direct puberty observation - fits Will's blunt style but somewhat predictable

jokes.text: Yeah, where'd she get that Lee Press-On body?
jokes.setup: Ashley's sudden physical transformation
jokes.punchline: Comparing her body to fake press-on nails
jokes.explanation: Clever product reference metaphor - comparing sudden curves to fake nails that can be applied instantly

jokes.text: Hurt me! Hurt me!
jokes.setup: Carlton seeing attractive Ashley
jokes.punchline: Repeat of his inappropriate sexual outburst
jokes.explanation: Running gag callback - same inappropriate response to attractive woman, less impact second time

jokes.text: I know you better float your midget boat on out of here.
jokes.setup: Will's pickup line about floating his boat
jokes.punchline: Ashley's height-based insult twist on the metaphor
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay callback and insult - Ashley turning Will's boat metaphor against him with height joke

jokes.text: Don't you shake your neck at me like that, girl.
jokes.setup: Ashley's sassy attitude toward Will
jokes.punchline: Will trying to assert authority with cultural reference to neck-shaking
jokes.explanation: Good cultural humor - Will invoking Black cultural behavior patterns to assert cousin authority

jokes.text: And don't you shake that either.
jokes.setup: Will telling Ashley not to shake her neck
jokes.punchline: Presumably telling her not to shake her hips/body either
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the shaking prohibition - Will trying to control Ashley's newfound confidence

jokes.text: You give a man a fish, you feed him for a day... But you give a man a cow and he can eat for a month, maybe more.
jokes.setup: Hilary trying to quote wisdom about self-sufficiency
jokes.punchline: Completely botching the famous saying with cow instead of teaching fishing
jokes.explanation: Excellent malapropism - perfect Hilary moment misunderstanding basic wisdom saying with absurd logic

jokes.text: Don't, don't, don't. She's on a roll.
jokes.setup: Hilary's mangled wisdom quote
jokes.punchline: Phil stopping others from correcting her to hear more nonsense
jokes.explanation: Good meta-commentary - Phil enjoying Hilary's intellectual disasters as entertainment

jokes.text: I'm going to live in the pool house. You said I had to be out of the house, but not off of the property.
jokes.setup: Hilary finding affordable housing solution
jokes.punchline: Exploiting literal interpretation of eviction terms
jokes.explanation: Excellent loophole logic - Hilary's rare moment of cleverness using literal interpretation to stay

jokes.text: Philip! Philip, honey. Honey, we're getting off easy. Come on. But it's not fair. She's winning!
jokes.setup: Hilary's pool house solution
jokes.punchline: Vivian stopping Phil from objecting because it's actually a good outcome for them
jokes.explanation: Good family dynamics - Vivian's practical acceptance vs Phil's competitive frustration

jokes.text: Baby, I'm telling you. You've got more moves than a bowl of Jell-O. And there's always room for Jell-O.
jokes.setup: Kenny trying to impress Ashley with smooth talk
jokes.punchline: Awkward Jell-O metaphor and slogan reference
jokes.explanation: Decent pickup line attempt combining food metaphor with advertising slogan - somewhat forced

jokes.text: Actually, Will, he sounds like you.
jokes.setup: Will calling Kenny a jackass for his pickup lines
jokes.punchline: Carlton pointing out Kenny learned from Will
jokes.explanation: Great turning-tables moment - Carlton pointing out Will's hypocrisy about pickup techniques

jokes.text: Actually, Will, he's better than you.
jokes.setup: Carlton's initial comparison of Kenny to Will
jokes.punchline: Upgrading Kenny above Will in pickup skills
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the insult - Carlton twisting the knife deeper about Will's dating skills

jokes.text: But, baby got back!
jokes.setup: Kenny complimenting Ashley's various attributes
jokes.punchline: Reference to Sir Mix-a-Lot song about buttocks
jokes.explanation: Predictable pop culture reference to famous rap song - fits era but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Will, I know my sister needs me, but that girl was babelicious.
jokes.setup: Carlton torn between family duty and attraction
jokes.punchline: Made-up word 'babelicious' expressing his dilemma
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Carlton's moral conflict with invented word that fits his nerdy speech

jokes.text: Carlton's a basket case.
jokes.setup: Will explaining Kenny and Carlton wanted the same type of girl
jokes.punchline: Dismissing Carlton as mentally unstable
jokes.explanation: Simple insult with good economy - Will's typical dismissal of Carlton's behavior

jokes.text: Actually, a lot of guys don't know. Well, really, damn near all guys don't know.
jokes.setup: Will trying to reassure Ashley that some guys appreciate quality
jokes.punchline: His reassurance escalating into admission that almost all guys are shallow
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation showing Will's honesty undermining his own pep talk - great character moment

jokes.text: Have your people call my people. We'll do lunch.
jokes.setup: Ashley returning to normal after Will's advice
jokes.punchline: Using Hollywood business clichés to say goodbye
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Ashley retaining some sophistication while returning to normal

jokes.text: people who live in glass cribs shouldn't throw hard stuff.
jokes.setup: Will trying to remember what Veronica said
jokes.punchline: Completely butchering 'people in glass houses shouldn't throw stones'
jokes.explanation: Good malapropism showing Will's nervousness - 'glass cribs' and 'hard stuff' are absurd substitutions

jokes.text: Let your smile be your umbrella?
jokes.setup: Will's continued failure to remember what Veronica said
jokes.punchline: Another completely unrelated saying as guess
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Will's desperation - throwing out random folksy sayings

jokes.text: You can't get it yet. But I've got your number.
jokes.setup: Will asking to write down her number
jokes.punchline: Veronica's wordplay about having his number (understanding him)
jokes.explanation: Smart wordplay on phone number vs. understanding someone - shows Veronica's intelligence

jokes.text: Am I as superficial as all those guys I warned Ashley about?
jokes.setup: Will has been checking out women all episode
jokes.punchline: His self-aware question about his own hypocrisy
jokes.explanation: Great self-aware moment showing character growth and recognition of hypocrisy

jokes.text: could it be that I'm more obsessed with breasts and thighs... than Colonel Sanders ever was?
jokes.setup: Will's self-reflection about being superficial
jokes.punchline: Comparing his objectification to KFC founder's chicken obsession
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay connecting sexual objectification to chicken parts - clever and quotable

jokes.text: Nah!
jokes.setup: Will's lengthy self-reflection about treating women better
jokes.punchline: Immediate rejection of personal growth with single word
jokes.explanation: Perfect anticlimactic punchline - long thoughtful buildup demolished by single word, pure character consistency

jokes.text: Yo, baby, I know your feet must be tired... 'cause you been running through my mind all day. Come here, girl!
jokes.setup: Will's return to his old pickup artist ways
jokes.punchline: Classic cheesy pickup line proving he learned nothing
jokes.explanation: Perfect character consistency callback - cheesy line proves his 'Nah!' moment was sincere

jokes.text: Any of y'all ever glossed your hair? Because I don't know. Maybe it's a new cult thing or something, that I don't know about.
jokes.setup: Carlton's confusion about 'glossing hair'
jokes.punchline: Asking audience and suggesting it might be cult activity
jokes.explanation: Decent stand-up audience interaction about malapropism - Carlton's confusion about beauty terms
